title: Honey Garlic Glazed Salmon
description: Perfectly seared and baked salmon fillets with a sweet and savory honey garlic glaze.
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Preheat your oven to 200°C (400°F).
In a small bowl, whisk together honey, soy sauce, lemon juice, and minced garlic.
Season salmon fillets with salt and pepper.
Heat olive oil in an oven-proof skillet and sear salmon skin-side up for 3 minutes.
Flip the salmon, pour the honey garlic sauce over it, and place the skillet in the oven.
Bake for 8-10 minutes until the salmon is cooked through and the sauce has thickened.
Spoon the glaze over the salmon and serve with lemon slices.

title: Warm Halloumi & Mushroom Salad
description: A fresh salad topped with seared halloumi cheese and mushrooms sautéed in teriyaki sauce.
instructions: Place the lettuce, cucumbers, tomatoes, and onion in a large salad bowl.
In a hot pan with a little oil, fry the halloumi cubes until golden brown on all sides, then remove.
Sauté the mushrooms in the same pan for about 3 minutes.
Return the halloumi to the pan, add the teriyaki sauce, and toss quickly for 30 seconds to coat.
Pour the warm pan contents over the fresh vegetables, sprinkle with sesame seeds, and serve immediately.

title: Beef Wellington Bites
description: A sophisticated appetizer featuring tender beef fillet wrapped in mushroom duxelles and flaky puff pastry.
instructions: Sear the beef chunks in a very hot pan for 30 seconds per side until browned but raw inside. Let cool.
In the same pan, sauté minced mushrooms and shallots until all moisture has evaporated (Duxelles).
Lay out plastic wrap, place prosciutto slices, spread the mushroom mixture, and place the beef on top. Roll tightly and chill for 15 minutes.
Wrap the chilled beef rolls in puff pastry, seal the edges, and brush with egg yolk.
Bake at 200°C for 15-20 minutes until the pastry is golden brown. Let rest before serving.
